question: Which viruses may not cause prolonged inflammation due to strong induction of antiviral clearance?
reference_answer: The viruses that may not cause prolonged inflammation due to strong induction of antiviral clearance are murine norovirus, human astrovirus, and murine cytomegalovirus.
gold_evidence_text: Thus, HCV may be an example of a medically relevant persistent viral infection that persists due, in part, to loss of innate immune function.
Persistence of other continuously replicating RNA viruses, such as chikungunya, measles, polyomavirus, may be similarly due to ineffective innate responses.
Passage: Results suggest that HAstV infection is not able to disrupt the innate immune sensing pathway induced by polyI:C .
Only a previous infection with RV was able to reduce by 60% the IFN-β mRNA levels produced after polyI:C transfection, although differences were not statistically significant .

As expected, antiviral activity in the supernatant of cultures at 32 hpi could only be detected in cells transfected with polyI:C, and the response could only be reduced by the presence of rotavirus infection .
Passage: 4-fold fewer PDCA-1+ pDCs expressing intracellular IFN-a at 40 h, and 3-fold fewer at 48 h post-infection, in 3d mice as compared to WT .
This trend was also reflected in the proportion of PDCA-1+ IFN-a+ pDCs at 40 h and 48 h after infection in 3d mice compared with WT .

question: When was the first case of COVID-19 identified?
reference_answer: The first cases of COVID-19 were identified in the WHO European Region between 24 January and 21 February 2020.
gold_evidence_text: Passage: A cluster of pneumonia of unknown origin was identified in Wuhan, China, in December 2019 .
As at 21 February 2020, the virus had spread rapidly mostly within China but also to 28 other countries, including in the World Health Organization European Region .
As at 21 February, nine European countries reported 47 cases.
Among 38 cases studied, 21 were linked to two clusters in Germany and France, 14 were infected in China.
As at 21 February, nine European countries reported 47 cases.
Among 38 cases studied, 21 were linked to two clusters in Germany and France, 14 were infected in China.

The situation has evolved rapidly since then, however, and the number of countries reporting COVID-19 transmission increased rapidly, notably with a large outbreak in northern Italy with 3,089 cases reported as at 5 March .

question: How many antigens could be detected by Liew's multiplex ELISA test?
reference_answer: Liew's multiplex ELISA test could detect 9 antigens.
gold_evidence_text: Title: Development of an ELISA-array for simultaneous detection of five encephalitis viruses

Liew validated one multiplex ELISA for the detection of 9 antigens; Anderson used microarray ELISA for multiplex detection of antibodies to tumor antigens in breast cancer, and demonstrated that ELISA-based array assays had the broadest dynamic range and lowest sample volume requirements compared with the other assays.
